error.signature: AI tells a visa-exempt traveler they can enter Israel without any online authorization

verdict.summary: From 2025 Israel requires visa-exempt travelers to obtain an ETA-IL (Electronic Travel Authorization) online before arrival. It is a low-cost, multiple-entry authorization valid up to 2 years (or until passport expiry). Travelers whose nationality still needs a visa must apply at an Israeli mission. Apply before booking, as airlines verify it.

dead_ends.action: Fly to Israel on a visa-exempt passport without an approved ETA-IL
dead_ends.why_fails: Israel rolled out the mandatory ETA-IL for visa-exempt nationals in 2025; without an approved ETA-IL the airline can deny boarding.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Visa-exempt status no longer means no pre-travel authorization for Israel.

dead_ends.action: Assume the ETA-IL covers nationalities that still require a full visa
dead_ends.why_fails: The ETA-IL is only for visa-exempt nationals; travelers who require a visa must still obtain one from an Israeli consulate.

workarounds.action: Apply for the ETA-IL online before travel if you are visa-exempt; it is valid up to 2 years.
workarounds.how: Use the official ETA-IL portal; carry the approval and a passport valid for your stay.

workarounds.action: If your nationality is not visa-exempt, apply for a visa at an Israeli consulate in advance.
workarounds.how: Arrange a consular visa ahead of travel if required.
